title: ferray-polynomial: no domain/window mapping — polynomials operate on raw x values
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's polynomial classes carry domain and window attributes. When evaluating or fitting, x values are mapped from domain to window via a linear transformation. This is critical for numerical stability — fitting a Chebyshev polynomial to data on [0, 1000] without mapping to [-1, 1] produces catastrophic cancellation. ferray-polynomial has no domain/window concept — evaluation and fitting use raw x values. All Poly trait methods (eval, fit, etc.) operate directly on the provided x without any mapping. Add domain: [f64; 2] and window: [f64; 2] fields to each polynomial type, with mapparms() and convert() methods.
